analysis_html:
Morning Intelligence Briefing — May 16

Kali Team Meeting — Elevate Services Enablement + Regional Pipeline Review

Attendees: Kali (RVP), Kevin, Jeff, Josh, Angela, Mel (Channel), Sean (Elevate Imp Manager), John (Managed Payroll), Andrew, Tommy (referenced)

Deal Status: This was not a single-deal meeting — it was a product enablement session on Elevate (Imp, Managed Payroll, Managed HR) plus a regional pipeline scrub. The region is significantly behind on FTAs (37 vs. 66 goal — ~56% of plan) and submitted revenue ($54K of $100K pin with only 10 selling days left). The biggest commercial blocker across the region is the inability to decouple Managed Payroll from Managed HR in CPQ until 7/1, which is causing discount friction and killing deals where clients want only one service. Tiered pricing is also not yet live in CPQ (June target). The team has strong broker enthusiasm for Elevate but reps lack confidence positioning it — no live client has gone through new-to-Paylocity implementation on Managed Payroll yet (only 90 internal conversions). Pipeline high for May is solid (~$347K from Jeff alone), but pin-to-submit conversion needs to accelerate immediately.

Key Decisions:

  • Partner reassignment rule set at 120-day no-contact threshold (free game); will funnel down to 90 days once established.
  • Business reviews deferred — will be either folded into 1:1s next week or scheduled as a Tuesday afternoon block.
  • Managed Payroll/HR decoupling in CPQ expected by end of next week per JD — Kali to escalate if needed before then.

Coaching Notes:

  • The team still has zero net-new Elevate Managed Payroll clients who came through full implementation — every competitive positioning claim is theoretical. Kali should prep a "what if the prospect asks for a reference?" contingency talk track before reps start pushing this harder. The "guinea pig" objection will keep surfacing until there's at least one referenceable win.
  • The 14% prime partner engagement rate is a structural problem, not an activity problem. The lift-and-shift cleanup is the right first move, but directors should audit whether reps even know what to say to prime partners beyond contests and webinar invites — the engagement gap may be a confidence issue, not a list-hygiene issue.

Next Steps:

  • 🔴 Kali — Escalate CPQ decoupling of Managed Payroll/HR if not live by end of next week; explore manual quoting workaround with John's team for any deal closing before 7/1 — by 5/22
  • Jeff — Follow up on managed services deal ($28K) pending approval to submit; confirm status and get submitted — today
  • John (Managed Payroll) — Connect with Rob Coosly on the pending intake form re: manual earnings/deductions question for the deal in queue — today
  • All Directors (Kevin, Jeff, Josh) — Send pin and high forecasts to Kali for next week; afternoon call blocks with reps at zero FTAs to diagnose blockers — today
  • Mel (Channel) — Email remaining team partner lift-and-shift lists; priority is clearing unknowns from prime partner space — by 5/22
  • Angela — Submit IST to reassign Bloom Golf and move to July; move Performance to July — no date — needs one

Market Intel:

  • Competitors — HCM Unlocked, Puzzle HR: Positioned as primary threats to Elevate services. Puzzle HR flagged for non-domestic employees, different working hours, and data privacy concerns. Key counter: they cannot be experts across hundreds of HCM systems; Paylocity team already knows the platform.
  • Managed Payroll pricing: $20 PEPM currently; tiered pricing (ranging $22-24 down to ~$5 for large accounts) rolling out in June. Discount guardrails: 15% max for majors employee count, up to 35% for larger. Heavily discounted product-side deals may trigger approval friction on Elevate pricing.
  • Unions: Hard no for Managed Payroll — one test client with 450 EEs consumes a full-time agent. Non-negotiable exclusion.
  • 401(k) update: All partner-referred accounts where the partner offers retirement services have been placed on a do-not-call list to protect broker relationships. The 401(k) product is reportedly selling well; simplified talk track from corporate is being distributed.

Pipeline Snapshot (Kali's Region — May):

  • Style Craft (Kevin) — Decision pushed to end of month; references all positive ("they told me they're going with you"). High confidence.
  • Quick timeline deal (Kevin) — Demo/pricing Monday, old relationship, wants July start. Likely submit next week.
  • Park Board of Trustees (Kevin) — Vendor of choice secured; governmental committee approval meeting 5/24. Final-week submit if timing holds.
  • Southern Pines (Jeff) — Good. Stays in May.
  • Central MS Planning (Jeff) — Pulled from May. Pushed.
  • Labor (Jeff) — Meeting Wednesday for answer.
  • Design Precast — Out of May (not a no, but client has too much going on).
  • Recenter — Paperwork sent; pinned for this week but didn't land. Stays in high.
  • Denim Price Group (Enterprise w/ Tommy) — Good. Stays in.
  • Strand & Lock (Angela) — Still high.
  • GlobeTek — High/pin/all slashes. Uncertain.
  • Sterling Automotive (Enterprise, Josh) — 50/50; top two finalist. Answer expected this month.

Josh's Team Meeting — 401(k) Enablement + Pipeline/Activity Review

Attendees: Josh (Director), Kevin (Retirement AE), Jay, Austin, Gabe, James, Rick, Ian (partial), Santino (BDR/ADP blitz), Angel (out — birthday), Jacob (out)

Deal Status: This was a team enablement and pipeline cadence meeting, not a single-deal review. The team is at $110K submitted for May (~70% to plan at midpoint) — strong pacing. Austin ($46K, 387% to plan) and Jacob ($53K, 190%) drove the month so far. Austin is now over plan for the year — 100 Club eligible. The team needs $240K between now and end of June to hit 100% as a group; pipeline supports it but execution has to hold. May pipeline is real and relatively clean. The 401(k) introduction via Kevin/Vestwell is a legitimate new revenue lever — $7 PEPM on participants, 100% stacked commission, three-month free promo live. The team is thinking about it but hasn't operationalized it into live deals yet. Demo Stack training landed well; reps are figuring out where it fits in their sales process — consensus is post-demo follow-ups, partner education, and FTA conversion.

Key Decisions:

  • 401(k) process established: warm intro email to Kevin + referral link; add Kevin to opportunity as 14% overlay + opportunity team member with read/write; for current clients, Kevin creates the op himself.
  • Managed Payroll/HR still cannot be sold separately until 7/1 — confirmed again.

Coaching Notes:

  • The 401(k) opportunity is real but risks going the way of every "nice to have" add-on — acknowledged in meetings but never executed. Josh should pick 2-3 specific open deals next week (James/Trinity with ADP is the obvious one) and have Kevin added to those opportunities as a forcing function. Waiting for reps to self-identify opportunities will delay adoption by weeks.

Next Steps:

  • 🔴 Josh — Identify 2-3 open ADP takeaway deals and have reps add Kevin as 14% overlay this week to pilot the 401(k) attach motion — no date — needs one
  • James — Check in with Santa Cruz next week for May signatures; Trinity Forest decision pushed to end of month — stay close — by 5/23
  • Gabe — Close POG this week; move North Texas Healthcare to June — by 5/16
  • All reps — Clean May pipeline: remove anything with no realistic path to May signature; update close dates by EOD — today
  • Santino (BDR) — DM team with ADP-focused pain-point questions; reps to respond with industry-specific ADP complaints and optionally send target accounts for cadence — next week
  • Austin — Connect Steven Amato's FA with the current 401(k) prospect; continue account cleanup — ongoing

Market Intel:

  • ADP vulnerability (from live deal): Payroll not speaking to time & labor; 401(k) with Fidelity not integrated with ADP; terrible reporting; terrible home screen/UX; poor customer service at scale. ADP Run clients are particularly vulnerable — small-business product, easiest displacement.
  • ADP platform fragmentation: Run (SMB), Workforce Now (mid-market), ADP Enterprise, TotalSource (PEO) — each is a different platform with different capabilities. Paylocity's single-platform story is a strong differentiator.
  • Vestwell 401(k) pricing: $500 setup, $250/mo base, $7/participant/mo. Basis fees start at 35 bps including investment advisory — well below industry 50-60 bps. Three-month free promo active (months 2-4 free, billing resumes month 5). JD has stated they will never lose on fees — heavy discounting authorized to win.
  • ADP/Paychex 401(k): Flagged as biggest flashing red lights for 401(k) conversion opportunity — subpar service + signals client likes all-in-one approach.

🔴 Director Priorities — Today

  1. 🔴 Escalate CPQ decoupling of Managed Payroll/HR. This is the single biggest commercial blocker across both teams. JD said end of next week — Kali should confirm a hard date in writing and have a manual quoting fallback ready for any deal that needs to close before 7/1. Every week this slips, it costs pipeline. Jeff's contact and Kali's direct line to JD should both be pressed today.
  2. 🔴 Rescue May FTA numbers on Kali's team. FTAs dropped from 53 to 37 week-over-week (~44% decline) with only 10 selling days left. Directors need afternoon call blocks today to diagnose what's stalling — pending crossover requests, scheduling gaps, or just inertia. The $54K submitted against a $100K pin with $28K managed services and $10K+ coming in means the math works, but only if submissions actually happen today and early next week.
  3. 🔴 Seed the 401(k) attach motion into 2-3 live deals. Both teams now have the enablement. Josh should tag Kevin into James's Trinity deal (ADP takeaway, obvious fit) and at least one other active ADP displacement this week. Kali's team should identify any current-client 401(k) opportunities from recent wins. The three-month free promo creates urgency to act now rather than "eventually."

📊 Pipeline Pulse

Josh's team is pacing strong — 70% to plan at midpoint with clean pipeline and two reps already over plan for the month. Kali's team has significant revenue sitting in pin/high ($190K+ from Jeff alone for next week) but submitted revenue is dangerously low relative to plan, and FTA activity fell off a cliff this week. The pattern across both teams: new revenue levers (Elevate, 401(k), Demo Stack) are being introduced and understood, but none have been operationalized into active deals yet — there's a real risk of "enablement without execution" if directors don't force specific deals into these motions in the next 7 days. The cross-team insight worth watching: ADP displacement is the highest-probability 401(k) attach opportunity, and Santino's ADP blitz creates a natural pipeline of exactly those accounts — connect Santino's outbound targeting with Kevin's 401(k) motion and you have a systematic revenue multiplier, not just two parallel initiatives.
